100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On July 12, 1926, the League of Nations held discussions regarding the ongoing tensions in the Far East, particularly focusing on the situation in China as conflicts continued to escalate. In the United States, the debate over Prohibition intensified, with various states considering measures to either enforce or repeal the Eighteenth Amendment. Meanwhile, in Europe, the aftermath of the recent economic downturn raised concerns about stability and recovery efforts across nations.
